popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
Life and career
William fought loyally for King Stephen of England, who made him first Earl of Arundel (more precisely, Earl of Sussex) (c. 1138[3]) and then Earl of Lincoln. In 1153 he helped arrange …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel於大約1099年出生在法國諾門迪曼其沙吉馬-達奧比尼時，他的父親William d'Aubigny37歲，母親Maud Bigod18歲。他於1138年與Adeliza de Louvain在英國素可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在英國被列為master butler of the royal household。他去世於1176年10月12日在英國英格蘭素瑞瓦維利 艾比享年78歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可維蒙達哈姆維蒙達哈姆 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel was born about 1099, in Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandy, France, his father, William d'Aubigny, was 37 and his mother, Maud Bigod, was 18. He married Adeliza de Louvain in 1138, in Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as master butler of the royal household in England. He died on 12 October 1176, in Waverley Abbey, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78, and was buried in Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel est né environ 1099, à Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandie, France, son père, William d'Aubigny, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Maud Bigod, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Adeliza de Louvain en 1138, à Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est master butler of the royal household à Angleterre. Il est décédé le 12 octobre 1176, à Waverley Abbey, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel um 1099 in Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater William d'Aubigny 37 und seine Mutter Maud Bigod 18. Er hat 1138 in Sussex, England Adeliza de Louvain geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als master butler of the royal household in England aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. Oktober 1176 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Waverley Abbey, Surrey, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel è nato il circa 1099 nel Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, William d'Aubigny, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Maud Bigod, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Adeliza de Louvain nel 1138, nel Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come master butler of the royal household nel England. Morì il 12 ottobre 1176, nel Waverley Abbey, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundelが約1099年にフランスノーマンディマンチェサイント-マルティン-ド'アウビグニーで生まれた時，父William d'Aubignyは37歳，母Maud Bigodは18歳であった。1138年にイギリススセックスでAdeliza de Louvainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。イギリスでの職業はmaster butler of the royal householdと記録されている。彼は1176年10月12日にイギリスイギリススリーワバーリー アビーで78歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクウイモンドハムウイモンドハム アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel(이)가 대략 1099년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 세인트-마틴-드'오비그니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William d'Aubigny(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Bigod(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1138년 영국 수세스에서 Adeliza de Louvain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국에서 master butler of the royal household이었다. 그는 1176년 10월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 수리 와벌리 애브브이에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 윈이몽함 윈이몽함 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel nasceu aproximadamente 1099, em Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandie, França, seu pai, William d'Aubigny, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Maud Bigod, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Adeliza de Louvain em 1138, em Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como master butler of the royal household em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 12 de outubro de 1176, em Waverley Abbey, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ro.Waverley Abbey, Surrey, England: Waverley Abbey, Surrey, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel родился около 1099 г. в местности сеинт-мартин-д'аубигни, манч, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William d'Aubigny было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Bigod, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Adeliza de Louvain в 1138 г. в местности суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии master butler of the royal household в местности Англия. Он умер 12 октября 1176 г. в местности вавирли эбби, сурри, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности вимондхам эбби, вимондхам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William d'Aubigny 1st Earl of Arundel nació aproximadamente 1099, en Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny, Manche, Normandie, Francia, su papá, William d'Aubigny, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Maud Bigod, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Adeliza de Louvain en 1138, en Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era master butler of the royal household en Inglaterra. Murió el 12 de octubre de 1176, en Waverley Abbey, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Wymondham Abbey, Wymondham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
